internal_communication_tools.name: Slack

rate_limits.posting_messages: 1 message per second per channel (with short burst allowance)

internal_communication_tools.auth_notes: Uses OAuth 2.0. Authentication requires a Bearer token passed in the 'Authorization' HTTP header. Tokens are scoped (e.g., 'chat:write', 'channels:read') and can represent either a Bot User or a User. Slack also supports 'Socket Mode' for apps behind firewalls, which uses a separate App-level token.

internal_communication_tools.name: Microsoft Teams (via Microsoft Graph)

rate_limits.general_teams_limit: 15,000 requests every 10 seconds per app per tenant

internal_communication_tools.auth_notes: Authenticated via Microsoft Entra ID (formerly Azure AD) using OAuth 2.0. Supports 'Delegated' permissions (acting on behalf of a signed-in user) and 'Application' permissions (acting as a background service). Requires a Tenant ID, Client ID, and Client Secret to obtain a JWT Bearer token.

internal_communication_tools.name: Zoom

rate_limits.heavy_api: 40 requests per second + 60,000 requests per day (Business+ plans)
rate_limits.resource_intensive: 20 requests per second + 60,000 requests per day (Business+ plans)

internal_communication_tools.auth_notes: Primarily uses OAuth 2.0 for user-level apps and 'Server-to-Server OAuth' for account-level integrations. Server-to-Server OAuth replaces the deprecated JWT auth and requires an Account ID, Client ID, and Client Secret. Tokens must be sent as a Bearer token in the Authorization header.

internal_communication_tools.name: Discord

rate_limits.invalid_request_limit: 10,000 per 10 minutes (results in temporary IP ban)
rate_limits.per_route_limits: Varies by bucket; typically 5 requests per 5 seconds for message-related routes

internal_communication_tools.auth_notes: Supports Bot Tokens and OAuth2 Bearer tokens. Bot tokens must be prefixed with 'Bot ' in the Authorization header (e.g., 'Authorization: Bot <token>'). OAuth2 tokens use the standard 'Bearer ' prefix. Rate limit state is returned in headers: X-RateLimit-Limit, X-RateLimit-Remaining, and X-RateLimit-Reset.

effort_recommendation.rationale: No native node - requires HTTP Request configuration
